questions.prompt: You're embedding a 10,000-word document for semantic search. Should you embed the whole document as one vector or chunk it first?

choices.text: Whole document — simpler and one vector is enough.
Chunk it into 500–800-token passages and embed each chunk separately. A single vector for 10,000 words averages out the meaning of multiple topics — queries about specific topics won't match well.

questions.explanation: Whole-document embeddings smear meaning across many topics, producing one diluted vector. Chunking lets each passage carry its own concentrated meaning, so queries about specific topics match the right passage. 500–800 tokens with some overlap is the typical sweet spot.

questions.prompt: Which embedding model is the sensible default for new RAG code in 2026?

choices.text: text-embedding-3-large at dimensions: 1536. Strong quality, fits HNSW index limits in pgvector, reasonable storage cost. Reduce dimensions further only when storage matters.
A custom embedding model trained from scratch.

questions.explanation: text-embedding-3-large is OpenAI's current strongest embedding model. At 1536 dims (configurable down from the default 3072), it fits cleanly into HNSW vector indexes and gets near the full-dimension quality. ada-002 is legacy. Custom training is rarely worth it.
